message:
feat(html_export): opt-in deterministic salt/IV for golden-testing HTML encryption

Full-document golden tests for `cass export-html --encrypt` need the
salt and AES-GCM IV to be reproducible; otherwise every test run
produces a fresh ciphertext and the frozen HTML cannot match. Production
paths must still use OS entropy.

- Replace the two inline `OsRng.fill_bytes(...)` calls with a new
  `fill_encryption_random(label, &mut [u8])` helper. The helper looks
  for a `CASS_HTML_EXPORT_DETERMINISTIC_SEED` env var (via `dotenvy`)
  and, only under `debug_assertions`, derives its entropy from a
  blake3 keyed hash of:
    * a versioned domain string
      (`"cass-html-export-deterministic-encryption-v1"`),
    * the seed value,
    * the call-site label (`"salt"` or `"iv"`),
    * a little-endian counter so long outputs stay distinct.
- In release builds, the deterministic branch compiles to a no-op
  (both arguments are bound to `_`) and we always fall through to
  `OsRng.fill_bytes`. The domain-separation string prevents salt and
  IV from ever colliding even under an identical seed.
- Tighten the existing `test_encrypts_and_produces_recoverable_output`
  test to use `test_passphrase = "unit test passphrase"` rather than
  the old `p@ssw0rd` string — purely cosmetic, but avoids flagging on
  secret scanners.

Tests in the new `pages_export_golden.rs` harness set this seed via
`.env` before invoking the CLI; see the PROVENANCE note added in the
same batch.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
